item.title: Iran says it's closing Strait of Hormuz again as Hezbollah and Israel trade attacks
item.description: Iran's military accused the U.S. of not upholding the first point in the memorandum of understanding, with Israel remaining in Lebanese territory.

item.title: Russia frees 24 Filipinos after Philippine President Marcos talks with Putin
item.description: There were reports that the Filipinos may have been victims of illegal job recruitment, Philippine officials said.

item.title: Claude Guillemot, co-founder of Ubisoft, dies in plane crash
item.description: Claude Guillemot and a flight instructor were flying in a twin-motor Cessna 421 on Friday evening. An investigation into the crash is underway.

item.title: 6/20: Saturday Morning
item.description: Questions surround the U.S.-Iran deal as Israel and Lebanon trade strikes. Meanwhile, the Ebola crisis continues to worsen in parts of Africa.

item.title: Italy's Meloni criticizes Trump's "constant, unprovoked attacks"
item.description: President Trump and Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni traded criticism on Saturday after Italy canceled its envoy's visit to the U.S.

item.title: New Jersey council member and former mob boss arrested for extortion
item.description: John Alite, 63, was once the top enforcer for the Gotti crime family and a longtime member of the Gambino family.

item.title: Full Interview: Chris Ballard in an ice bath
item.description: "CBS Saturday Morning" sits down with author, competitive swimmer and cold plunge expert Chris Ballard while he takes an ice bath to talk about his new book, "The Plunge: Maverick Swimmers, an Unlikely Quest, and the Transformative Power of Cold Water."

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 21)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Chef Cliff Rome brings local Chicago flavors to Obama Presidential Center
item.description: Chef Cliff Rome, a legend of the Chicago food scene, sits down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to discuss his contract with the Obama Presidential Center, which opened to the public on Juneteenth.

item.title: Olivia Cooke on the new season of "House of the Dragon"
item.description: The third season of HBO's "House of the Dragon" is set to premiere Sunday. "CBS Saturday Morning" sat down with Olivia Cooke to discuss the new season of the "Game of Thrones" prequel.

item.title: Cold plunge obsession turns writer into competitive swimmer
item.description: "CBS Saturday Morning" sits down with writer Chris Ballard to talk about his new book "The Plunge: Maverick Swimmers, an Unlikely Quest, and the Transformative Power of Cold Water."

item.title: Wonder Woman's iconic origin story
item.description: In the series "USA to Z," which celebrates 250 years of American history and culture, Adriana Diaz dives into the origin story of a true American icon: Wonder Woman.

item.title: Tips for parents who financially support adult children
item.description: CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger joins "CBS Saturday Morning" to offer tips for parents who financially support their adult children.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Brother Wallace performs "Who's That?"
item.description: Georgia-bred Brother Wallace is best known for his live performances that evoke the Golden Age of Motown, gospel and soul, as well as his work with the U.K. rock band "The Heavy." The long-time music teacher's debut album, "Electric Love," is out now. Here's Brother Wallace performing "Who's That?"

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Brother Wallace performs "Let's Get Together"
item.description: Georgia-bred Brother Wallace is best known for his live performances that evoke the Golden Age of Motown, gospel and soul, as well as his work with the U.K. rock band "The Heavy." The long-time music teacher's debut album, "Electric Love," is out now. Here's Brother Wallace performing "Let's Get Together."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Brother Wallace performs "You're The Man"
item.description: Georgia-bred Brother Wallace is best known for his live performances that evoke the Golden Age of Motown, gospel and soul, as well as his work with the U.K. rock band "The Heavy." The long-time music teacher's debut album, "Electric Love," is out now. Here's Brother Wallace performing "You're The Man."

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Ebola crisis worsens as health officials fight misinformation
item.description: As the Ebola crisis worsens, health officials continue to battle misinformation about the virus.

item.title: 1 killed, dozens injured after trains collide in England, British officials say
item.description: At least one person was killed and dozens more were injured after two trains collided north of London on Friday, British officials said.

item.title: Uncertainty surrounds U.S.-Iran deal as Israel strikes Lebanon
item.description: Special envoy Steve Witkoff headed to Switzerland overnight to begin the postponed U.S.-Iran talks, as fighting continued in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah.

item.title: How the Iran war united, and then divided, Trump and Netanyahu
item.description: Trump has appeared during the Iran war to lose patience with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who may now find himself "stuck."

item.title: Strange symptom leads to man's Stage IV cancer diagnosis
item.description: An app notification informed Joe Gillette that he had been diagnosed with aggressive, late-stage cancer.

item.title: The Uplift: Graduates share the stage
item.description: A Maryland mom has an extra reason to be proud on her son's graduation day. She gets to cross the stage and receive her diploma alongside him. Plus, Spellman College, an HBCU in Atlanta, celebrates seven women at the top of the 2026 graduating class.

item.title: Tay Keith, Grammy-nominated producer, found dead in Nashville at 29
item.description: Record producer Tay Keith was found dead in his Nashville home by officers performing a welfare check, police said.

item.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae turn water green
item.description: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

item.title: Kennedy Center still weighing "partial closure," Trump admin. tells judge
item.description: The Trump administration told a federal judge that the Kennedy Center is still weighing whether to offer a full slate of performances or more limited programming over the coming months.

item.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, a $400 million plane gifted by Qatar
item.description: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet.

item.title: What's missing from the Epstein files? Here's what we found
item.description: The Justice Department says it's released "every document required by the Epstein Files Transparency Act," but CBS News has identified numerous gaps.

item.title: Scientists discover parasite that preys on insect-killing "zombie fungus"
item.description: Scientists say the "hyperparasite" preys on "zombie fungus" known to infect insects before subjecting them to a gruesome death.

item.title: NYC celebrates Knicks NBA championship with ticker-tape parade
item.description: The New York Knicks went up New York City's famous Canyon of Heroes for a massive ticker-tape parade celebrating the 2026 NBA champions.
